threads.title: Data model: add additions, deletions, caption to Hunk and hunk_captions to Walkthrough
threads.summary: Introduces three new fields on `Hunk` (`additions`, `deletions`, `caption`) and a new top-level `hunk_captions: dict[str, str]` on `Walkthrough`. All default safely so old cached walkthroughs deserialise without error. The JSON schema is updated to require `hunk_captions` from the LLM response.
threads.root_cause: The feature needs a place in the data model to carry per-hunk diff counters (computed at parse time) and per-hunk captions (generated by the LLM). Everything else in the PR depends on these fields existing.

hunks.content:
"context_before": self.context_before,

hunks.caption: Hunk.to_dict new fields

steps.narration: Three new fields are appended to `Hunk`: `additions` (int, default 0), `deletions` (int, default 0), and `caption` (str, default ""). All are optional with safe defaults so existing Hunk construction sites need no changes, and old cache payloads that predate these fields still deserialise cleanly via `from_dict`.

hunks.caption: Walkthrough.hunk_captions field
Walkthrough.to_dict hunk_captions
Walkthrough.from_dict hunk_captions

steps.narration: `Walkthrough` gains a `hunk_captions: dict[str, str]` field (defaults to `{}`). `to_dict`, `from_dict`, and the JSON schema are all updated in lockstep. Notably, `from_dict` uses `.get("hunk_captions", {})` so any pre-existing cached walkthroughs continue to load — they just have an empty map, which the rest of the code treats as captions not available.

steps.narration: Model-level tests verify that the three new `Hunk` fields round-trip through `to_dict`/`from_dict` and that old payloads missing those keys correctly default to zero/empty. Two `Walkthrough` tests do the same for `hunk_captions`.

threads.title: Compute additions/deletions counts during diff parsing
threads.summary: Inside `parse_diff`, the line-level loop now increments `additions` and `deletions` counters using `unidiff`'s `is_added`/`is_removed` predicates and passes them into the `Hunk` constructor. Counters are always recomputed from raw diff content on every load, making them immune to stale cache values.
threads.root_cause: The TUI needs accurate +X/-Y counts for each hunk. Rather than storing a static number in the cache, the counters are derived live from the parsed diff so they stay consistent even if the model schema evolves.

+            additions = 0
+            deletions = 0
             for line in hunk:
                 content_lines.append(str(line))
+                if line.is_added:
+                    additions += 1
+                elif line.is_removed:
+                    deletions += 1
             hunks.append(
                 Hunk(
                     file_path=file_path,
hunks.caption: Additions/deletions counting loop
Pass additions/deletions to Hunk

steps.narration: Two counters (`additions`, `deletions`) are initialised before the per-line loop and incremented based on `line.is_added` and `line.is_removed`. They are then forwarded to the `Hunk(...)` constructor call.

threads.title: Instruct the LLM to produce a hunk_captions map
threads.summary: A new '## Hunk captions' section is added to the system prompt, explaining the expected format (2-5 word one-liners, not why but what), providing concrete examples, and making coverage mandatory. The test fixture is updated to include a sample `hunk_captions` map so downstream deserialization tests have realistic data.
threads.root_cause: Without explicit prompt instructions, the LLM has no guidance to produce the `hunk_captions` map required by the updated JSON schema. The prompt section also doubles as specification documentation read by both the LLM and human contributors.

hunks.content:
exactly as given — do NOT invent line numbers or new IDs. Each step's `hunks` \
 field is an array of these string IDs.
 
+## Hunk captions
+
+Provide a top-level `hunk_captions` object mapping every hunk ID to a short \
+one-liner (2-5 words) describing **what** the hunk contains at a glance — \
+not why. Examples: "New imports", "Imports update", "Removed imports", \
+"New createFooBar function", "getFooBar signature update", \
+"bar sorting order update", "New constants", "Constants update", \
+"New X enum item", "Removed dead code". Coverage is mandatory: every hunk ID \
+in the File Summary must appear as a key in `hunk_captions`. The caption \
+describes the hunk content itself, so it does not change based on which \
+step references the hunk.
+
 ## Guiding principles
 
 1. **Coverage is mandatory**: Every hunk ID listed in the File Summary MUST appear \
hunks.caption: Prompt hunk captions section

steps.narration: The system prompt gains a dedicated 'Hunk captions' section before the 'Guiding principles' block. It specifies the format (2-5 words, describes what not why), supplies eight examples, and reinforces that every hunk ID in the File Summary must appear as a key.

hunks.caption: Fixture hunk_captions addition

steps.narration: The `sample_response.json` fixture is updated to include a `hunk_captions` object with three entries. This keeps the fixture consistent with the updated schema and feeds the model/hydration tests that deserialise it.

threads.title: Propagate new Hunk fields through hydration and warn on missing captions
threads.summary: The `hydrate_walkthrough` function now copies `additions`, `deletions`, and `caption` from the parsed `Hunk` into each hydrated copy. It also implements a nuanced warning strategy: if `hunk_captions` is entirely absent (old cache), it stays silent; if some captions exist but others are missing, it emits a single consolidated warning listing the missing IDs.
threads.root_cause: Hydration creates fresh `Hunk` copies to avoid mutating shared state. Without explicit propagation, the new fields would silently be zero/empty in every hydrated hunk. The partial-caption warning catches LLM responses that provide some but not all captions.

hunks.content:
"""
     warnings: list[str] = []
     by_id = {h.id: h for h in parsed_hunks if h.id}
+    # Captions are best-effort. If the walkthrough was produced before captions
+    # existed (e.g. an old cache), the entire map is empty — that's expected,
+    # don't warn. Only flag missing captions when some are present, and even
+    # then collapse to a single summary line so we don't flood stdout.
+    has_any_captions = bool(walkthrough.hunk_captions)
+    missing_captions: list[str] = []
 
     for thread in walkthrough.threads:
         for step in thread.steps:
hunks.caption: Caption tracking setup

steps.narration: Before iterating threads, `has_any_captions` flags whether the walkthrough has any captions at all, and `missing_captions` will collect IDs that are absent. This distinction enables the silent-on-old-cache / warn-on-partial behaviour.

hunks.caption: Caption lookup and missing tracking

steps.narration: The `Hunk(...)` constructor call now forwards `additions`, `deletions`, and `caption`. After the loop, if any captions were missing, a single consolidated warning is appended listing all IDs in one line rather than one warning per hunk.

+    def test_missing_caption_warns_with_summary(self, simple_diff: str):
+        # Partial captions: H1 has one, H2 doesn't. Should produce a single
+        # summary warning naming the missing IDs (not one per hunk).
+        parsed = parse_diff(simple_diff)
+        first, second = parsed[0], parsed[1]
+
+        wt = Walkthrough(
+            threads=[
+                Thread(
+                    id="t1",
+                    title="T1",
+                    summary="s",
+                    root_cause="r",
+                    steps=[
+                        ThreadStep(
+                            hunks=[Hunk(id=first.id), Hunk(id=second.id)],
+                            narration="test",
+                            order=1,
+                        )
+                    ],
+                )
+            ],
+            overview="test",
+            suggested_order=["t1"],
+            hunk_captions={first.id: "Init guard"},
+        )
+
+        wt, warnings = hydrate_walkthrough(wt, parsed)
+        caption_warnings = [w for w in warnings if "caption" in w]
+        assert len(caption_warnings) == 1
+        assert second.id in caption_warnings[0]
+        assert first.id not in caption_warnings[0]
+
+    def test_empty_hunk_captions_does_not_warn(self, simple_diff: str):
+        # Old-cache behavior: an entirely empty hunk_captions dict means the
+        # walkthrough predates captions; we should silently render without them

hunks.caption: New hydration caption tests

steps.narration: Three hydration tests cover the new behaviour: additions/deletions/caption are correctly copied into the hydrated hunk; partial captions produce a single summarised warning naming only the missing IDs; an entirely empty `hunk_captions` (old-cache path) produces no warnings.

threads.title: Render captions and +X-Y counters in both TUI views
threads.summary: The thread-walkthrough view (`_render_thread_rows`) and the full-diff view (`_render_full_diff`) both gain caption rows (dimmed, flips to `reverse white` when focused) above each hunk row, and inline +X-Y counters (green/red) between the path and the (lines …) annotation. The full-diff file header also sums counters across all its hunks. Caption rows are purely visual and do not consume `row_cursor` slots.
threads.root_cause: A long list of hunks from the same file was hard to distinguish. Captions give each hunk a human-readable label at a glance; counters give immediate size context without expanding the hunk.

hunks.caption: Caption row in thread view

steps.narration: In `_render_thread_rows`, a caption `Text` row is prepended (indented 4 spaces) when `hunk.caption` is non-empty. It uses `dim` style normally and `reverse white` when the hunk row is focused, keeping the caption visually attached to its hunk during keyboard navigation. Crucially, this row is pushed into `parts` but does NOT advance `row_cursor`, so focus indexing is unaffected.

from rich.text import Text
 
 from unravel.config import DiffDisplayConfig
-from unravel.models import Hunk
+from unravel.models import Hunk, Thread, ThreadStep, Walkthrough
+from unravel.tui.state import WalkthroughState
 from unravel.tui.widgets.page_content import (
     _ADD_BG,
     _DEL_BG,
+    _render_full_diff,
     _render_hunk_diff,
+    _render_thread_rows,
     _resolve_language,

table = panel.renderable
         code_cells = list(table.columns[-1]._cells)
         assert len(code_cells[0].spans) > 0
+
+
+class TestRenderThreadRows:
+    def _state_with(self, hunks: list[Hunk]) -> WalkthroughState:
+        thread = Thread(
+            id="t1",
+            title="T1",
+            summary="s",
+            root_cause="r",
+            steps=[ThreadStep(hunks=hunks, narration="n", order=1)],
+        )
+        wt = Walkthrough(
+            threads=[thread],
+            overview="ov",
+            suggested_order=["t1"],
+        )
+        state = WalkthroughState(walkthrough=wt)
+        state.page_index = 1
+        return state
+
+    def _texts(self, parts):
+        return [p for p in parts if isinstance(p, Text)]
+
+    def test_caption_row_appears_above_hunk(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H1",
+            file_path="src/foo.py",
+            new_start=10,
+            new_count=5,
+            additions=2,
+            deletions=1,
+            caption="New imports",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h])
+        parts = _render_thread_rows(state, state.ordered_threads[0])
+        texts = self._texts(parts)
+        # Find the caption row immediately followed by the file row.
+        plains = [t.plain for t in texts]
+        caption_idx = next(
+            i for i, p in enumerate(plains) if p.strip() == "New imports"
+        )
+        file_idx = next(
+            i for i, p in enumerate(plains) if "src/foo.py" in p and "▶" in p
+        )
+        assert caption_idx == file_idx - 1
+
+    def test_caption_row_omitted_when_empty(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H1",
+            file_path="src/foo.py",
+            new_start=10,
+            new_count=5,
+            additions=2,
+            deletions=1,
+            caption="",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h])
+        parts = _render_thread_rows(state, state.ordered_threads[0])
+        plains = [t.plain for t in self._texts(parts)]
+        # No bare caption-style row should sit above the file row.
+        file_idx = next(i for i, p in enumerate(plains) if "src/foo.py" in p)
+        # The line directly above is either the step line, blank, or another row;
+        # it must not be a stray caption-only string.
+        above = plains[file_idx - 1]
+        assert above.strip() == "" or "Step" in above or "▶" in above
+
+    def test_diff_counter_segments_have_green_red_styles(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H1",
+            file_path="src/foo.py",
+            new_start=10,
+            new_count=5,
+            additions=4,
+            deletions=30,
+            caption="Imports update",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h])
+        parts = _render_thread_rows(state, state.ordered_threads[0])
+        file_text = next(
+            t for t in self._texts(parts) if "src/foo.py" in t.plain
+        )
+        # The plain text contains both segments adjacent: "+4-30".
+        assert "+4-30" in file_text.plain
+        # Verify color spans cover those segments.
+        styles = [str(span.style) for span in file_text.spans]
+        assert any(s == "green" for s in styles)
+        assert any(s == "red" for s in styles)
+
+    def test_counter_omitted_when_zero(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H1",
+            file_path="empty.bin",
+            new_start=0,
+            new_count=0,
+            additions=0,
+            deletions=0,
+            caption="Binary file",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h])
+        parts = _render_thread_rows(state, state.ordered_threads[0])
+        file_text = next(
+            t for t in self._texts(parts) if "empty.bin" in t.plain
+        )
+        assert "+0" not in file_text.plain
+        assert "-0" not in file_text.plain
+
+    def test_caption_rows_do_not_consume_focus_index(self):
+        # Two hunks with captions — current_rows() must still return 2 rows
+        # (one per hunk), not 4.
+        h1 = Hunk(
+            id="H1", file_path="a.py", new_start=1, new_count=2,
+            additions=1, deletions=0, caption="Cap A",
+        )
+        h2 = Hunk(
+            id="H2", file_path="b.py", new_start=1, new_count=2,
+            additions=0, deletions=1, caption="Cap B",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h1, h2])
+        rows = state.current_rows()
+        assert len(rows) == 2
+
+
+class TestRenderFullDiff:
+    def _state_with(self, parsed: list[Hunk], captions: dict[str, str]) -> WalkthroughState:
+        thread = Thread(
+            id="t1",
+            title="T1",
+            summary="s",
+            root_cause="r",
+            steps=[
+                ThreadStep(
+                    hunks=[Hunk(id=h.id) for h in parsed],
+                    narration="n",
+                    order=1,
+                )
+            ],
+        )
+        wt = Walkthrough(
+            threads=[thread],
+            overview="ov",
+            suggested_order=["t1"],
+            hunk_captions=captions,
+        )
+        state = WalkthroughState(walkthrough=wt, all_hunks=parsed)
+        # Move to the full-diff page (last page).
+        state.page_index = state.page_count - 1
+        return state
+
+    def _texts_from_group(self, group):
+        return [r for r in group.renderables if isinstance(r, Text)]
+
+    def test_full_diff_shows_caption_above_row(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H1",
+            file_path="src/foo.py",
+            new_start=10,
+            new_count=5,
+            additions=2,
+            deletions=1,
+            content=" ctx\n+a\n-b\n",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h], {"H1": "New imports"})
+        result = _render_full_diff(state)
+        plains = [t.plain for t in self._texts_from_group(result)]
+        cap_idx = next(i for i, p in enumerate(plains) if p.strip() == "New imports")
+        row_idx = next(
+            i
+            for i, p in enumerate(plains)
+            if "H1" in p and ("▶" in p or "▼" in p)
+        )
+        assert cap_idx == row_idx - 1
+
+    def test_full_diff_shows_diff_counter(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H2",
+            file_path="src/bar.py",
+            new_start=20,
+            new_count=3,
+            additions=4,
+            deletions=30,
+            content=" ctx\n",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h], {"H2": "Constants update"})
+        result = _render_full_diff(state)
+        row_text = next(
+            t
+            for t in self._texts_from_group(result)
+            if "H2" in t.plain and ("▶" in t.plain or "▼" in t.plain)
+        )
+        assert "+4-30" in row_text.plain
+        styles = [str(span.style) for span in row_text.spans]
+        assert any(s == "green" for s in styles)
+        assert any(s == "red" for s in styles)
+
+    def test_full_diff_omits_counter_when_zero(self):
+        h = Hunk(
+            id="H3",
+            file_path="img.png",
+            new_start=0,
+            new_count=0,
+            additions=0,
+            deletions=0,
+            content="[binary file]",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h], {"H3": "Binary asset"})
+        result = _render_full_diff(state)
+        row_text = next(
+            t
+            for t in self._texts_from_group(result)
+            if "H3" in t.plain and ("▶" in t.plain or "▼" in t.plain)
+        )
+        assert "+0" not in row_text.plain
+        assert "-0" not in row_text.plain
+
+    def test_full_diff_file_header_aggregates_counters(self):
+        # Two hunks in the same file — the file header should sum their
+        # additions and deletions and show them between the path and (N hunks).
+        h1 = Hunk(
+            id="H1", file_path="src/foo.py", new_start=10, new_count=3,
+            additions=4, deletions=1, content=" ctx\n",
+        )
+        h2 = Hunk(
+            id="H2", file_path="src/foo.py", new_start=30, new_count=5,
+            additions=6, deletions=2, content=" ctx\n",
+        )
+        state = self._state_with([h1, h2], {"H1": "A", "H2": "B"})
+        result = _render_full_diff(state)
+        header = next(
+            t
+            for t in self._texts_from_group(result)
+            if t.plain.startswith("src/foo.py") and "hunks" in t.plain
+        )
+        assert "+10" in header.plain
+        assert "-3" in header.plain
+        assert "(2 hunks)" in header.plain
+        styles = [str(span.style) for span in header.spans]
+        assert any(s == "green" for s in styles)
+        assert any(s == "red" for s in styles)
hunks.caption: New page_content tests

steps.narration: Test infrastructure is extended: `_render_full_diff` and `_render_thread_rows` are exported from `page_content.py` so they can be called directly in tests. Two new test classes cover: caption appears directly above the hunk row; caption omitted when empty; counters show green/red spans; counters absent when both are zero; caption rows do not inflate the focus-row count; file headers aggregate counters correctly.

threads.title: CLI: Rich help panels, option reordering, and -h <cmd> entrypoint fix
threads.summary: CLI help output is improved by grouping options into labelled Rich panels (Output format, Cache control, Model & API, Source) and adding missing short flags (-f/--fresh, -s/--staged, -r/--remote). A new `entrypoint()` wrapper replaces the raw `app` entry point to fix the case where `unravel -h pr` shows root help instead of pr help. `pyproject.toml` is updated to point to `entrypoint`.
threads.root_cause: Users found the flat option list hard to scan and discovered that `unravel -h <cmd>` did not show per-command help because Click treats `-h` as an eager root option before any subcommand parser runs. The `entrypoint` wrapper detects and rewrites the argument order before handing off to Typer.

hunks.content:
render_tree,
 )
 
+_CTX = {"help_option_names": ["-h", "--help"]}
+
+# Rich help panel labels — keep in module scope so the same label is reused
+# verbatim across commands (Typer groups by exact string).
+_PANEL_OUTPUT = "Output format"
+_PANEL_CACHE = "Cache control"
+_PANEL_MODEL = "Model & API"
+_PANEL_SOURCE = "Source"
+
 app = typer.Typer(
     name="unravel",
-    help="AI-powered CLI that decomposes PR diffs into causal threads.",
+    help=(
+        "AI-powered CLI that decomposes PR diffs into causal threads.\n\n"
+        "Run [bold]unravel COMMAND -h[/bold] (or [bold]unravel -h COMMAND[/bold]) "
+        "to see options for a specific command — e.g. [cyan]unravel pr -h[/cyan]."
+    ),
     no_args_is_help=True,
+    context_settings=_CTX,
+    rich_markup_mode="rich",
 )
 cache_app = typer.Typer(
     name="cache",
     help="Manage the local analysis cache.",
     no_args_is_help=True,
+    context_settings=_CTX,
 )
 app.add_typer(cache_app, name="cache")
 conf_app = typer.Typer(
     name="conf",
     help="View and edit persistent Unravel settings.",
     invoke_without_command=True,
+    context_settings=_CTX,
 )
 app.add_typer(conf_app, name="conf")
 console = Console(stderr=True)
hunks.caption: CLI constants and app config

steps.narration: Module-level constants are added: `_CTX` holds `help_option_names` so `-h` triggers help everywhere, and four `_PANEL_*` string constants name the Rich help panel labels. The `app`, `cache_app`, and `conf_app` Typer instances are updated to use `_CTX` and enable `rich_markup_mode="rich"`.

hunks.caption: pr command model/cache options

steps.narration: The `diff` and `pr` commands have their option lists reorganised: source-related options (`--staged`, `--remote`) move to the top of the signature, followed by output options, then cache options, and finally model/API options. Each `typer.Option(...)` call gains a `rich_help_panel` kwarg pointing to the appropriate panel constant. New short flags (-f for --fresh, -s for --staged, -r for --remote) are added.

hunks.content:
f"[red]Unexpected error:[/red] {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
         )
         raise typer.Exit(1) from exc
+
+
+def entrypoint() -> None:
+    """Console-script entry point.
+
+    Rewrites ``unravel -h <cmd> [...]`` to ``unravel <cmd> [...] -h`` so the
+    inverse ordering shows per-command help. Click/Typer treats ``-h`` /
+    ``--help`` as an eager option that fires before any subcommand parser
+    runs, so without this swap ``unravel -h pr`` would just print the root
+    help.
+    """
+    import sys
+
+    args = sys.argv[1:]
+    if args and args[0] in ("-h", "--help") and len(args) > 1 and not args[1].startswith("-"):
+        sys.argv = [sys.argv[0], *args[1:], args[0]]
+    app()
hunks.caption: New entrypoint wrapper function

steps.narration: `entrypoint()` is a thin wrapper: it inspects `sys.argv`, and if the first argument is `-h`/`--help` followed by a non-flag word (a subcommand name), it swaps them — converting `unravel -h pr` to `unravel pr -h` before calling `app()`. This makes per-command help work intuitively in both orderings.

hunks.content:
]
 
 [project.scripts]
-unravel = "unravel.cli:app"
+unravel = "unravel.cli:entrypoint"
 
 [tool.hatch.version]
 source = "vcs"
hunks.caption: Entry point renamed to entrypoint

steps.narration: `pyproject.toml` updates the `[project.scripts]` entry from `unravel.cli:app` to `unravel.cli:entrypoint` so the installed `unravel` binary goes through the new wrapper.

overview: This PR adds per-hunk diff counters (+X-Y in green/red) and LLM-generated short captions to the walkthrough TUI. Captions are sourced from a new top-level `hunk_captions` map on the walkthrough JSON, which the LLM is instructed to populate. Counters are derived from `unidiff` at parse time. Alongside the core feature, CLI help output is improved with Rich-grouped option panels and a new `entrypoint` wrapper that fixes `unravel -h <cmd>` ordering. All 202 tests pass with new coverage across models, git, hydration, and TUI rendering.

source.title: Add per-hunk diff counters and captions to walkthrough TUI
